system_prompt: You are an analyst. Read the brief carefully and provide your best-reasoned recommendation.
user_prompt:
You are presented with 5 anonymous responses (E1-E5) and 15 bipolar constructs. Rate each response on each construct on a 1-7 scale: 1 = strongly the LEFT pole, 7 = strongly the RIGHT pole, 4 = neutral or mixed.

Responses:
**E1:** ## Recommendation: Option A — "Mastery"

Launch as the serious learner's tool. With one shot at positioning a $1200 novel device, the team's scarcest asset is not TAM — it's credibility. A tactile-AI woodworking trainer is the kind of product that will be dismissed as a gimmick by exactly the people whose endorsement determines whether it survives year two.

**E2:** # Recommendation: Option A — "Mastery"

Launch as the serious learner's tool. Here's why this is the only choice that compounds. A novel $1200 device with no direct competitor faces one existential threat: being misunderstood.

**E3:** ## Recommendation: Option A — "Mastery"

Launch as the serious learner's tool. Here's why this is the right bet despite the small TAM headline. A novel $1200 device with no competitor faces one existential threat: being dismissed as a gimmick.

**E4:** # Recommendation: Option A — "Mastery"

Launch as the serious learner's tool. Here's why this is the right bet despite the apparent TAM problem. A novel $1200 device with no competitor faces one existential risk above all others: being misunderstood.

**E5:** # Recommendation: Option A — "Mastery"

Launch as the serious learner's tool. This is the only framing that lets the product's actual capabilities — real-time tactile guidance through joinery — do the heavy lifting of the marketing. Every other framing requires the buyer to believe a story *adjacent* to the product; Mastery requires them to believe the product itself.

Constructs (id : left pole ↔ right pole):
  C_M1_1: 'Frames argument around defensive risk-avoidance (gimmick dismissal)'  (1) ↔ (7)  'Frames argument around offensive product-capability alignment'
  C_M1_2: 'Emphasizes credibility and expert endorsement as scarce asset'  (1) ↔ (7)  'Emphasizes avoiding misunderstanding as existential threat'
  C_M1_3: 'Concise standalone claim without elaboration scaffolding'  (1) ↔ (7)  'Uses explanatory bridge phrasing to justify the pick'
  C_M2_1: 'Frames risk as being misunderstood (E2, E4)'  (1) ↔ (7)  'Frames argument around product capability fit (E5)'
  C_M2_2: 'Defends against TAM-size objection explicitly (E3, E4)'  (1) ↔ (7)  'Argues from product-marketing alignment (E5)'
  C_M2_3: 'Centers credibility and gimmick-dismissal risk (E1, E4)'  (1) ↔ (7)  'Centers product's intrinsic capabilities as proof (E5)'
  C_M3_1: 'Frames risk as being dismissed as gimmick (E1, E3)'  (1) ↔ (7)  'Frames risk as being misunderstood (E4)'
  C_M3_2: 'Opens with concise thesis sentence before reasoning (E3, E4)'  (1) ↔ (7)  'Opens by emphasizing compounding strategic logic (E2)'
  C_M3_3: 'Emphasizes credibility and gimmick-dismissal threat (E1, E3)'  (1) ↔ (7)  'Emphasizes compounding value over product lifetime (E2)'
  C_M4_1: 'Frames choice around defensive risk avoidance (E2, E4)'  (1) ↔ (7)  'Frames choice around offensive capability showcase (E5)'
  C_M4_2: 'Emphasizes compounding or product-led marketing logic (E2, E5)'  (1) ↔ (7)  'Emphasizes avoiding gimmick dismissal despite small TAM (E3)'
  C_M4_3: 'Names threat as misunderstanding (E2, E4)'  (1) ↔ (7)  'Names threat as being dismissed as gimmick (E3)'
  C_M5_1: 'Frames choice as defensive risk mitigation'  (1) ↔ (7)  'Frames choice as offensive capability showcase'
  C_M5_2: 'Emphasizes credibility and gatekeeper endorsement'  (1) ↔ (7)  'Emphasizes abstract strategic compounding logic'
  C_M5_3: 'Centers external audience perception risk'  (1) ↔ (7)  'Centers internal product-message coherence'

OUTPUT FORMAT (strict). Write exactly 75 lines, no commentary, no markdown fences, no headers. One line per (construct, element) pair, in this exact format:
<construct_id>,<element>,<rating>

Example (showing the format only - your numbers will differ):
C_M1_1,E1,1
C_M1_1,E2,2
C_M1_1,E3,3
C_M1_2,E1,4
...

Constraints:
- rating is an integer from 1 to 7
- iterate all 15 constructs in the order listed, and within each construct all 5 elements in order (E1-E5)
- produce 75 lines total
- output ONLY the lines, nothing before or after
